purpose: Business-decision-based question group configuration for Step 4 AI-guided use case intake.

global_user_experience_policy.primary_rule: Each active question must ask one clear business decision, not a list of unrelated missing fields.
global_user_experience_policy.hidden_internal_changes: Dependency-driven status/applicability changes must go to InternalImpactPlan and must not be shown as user-facing Action Preview items.
global_user_experience_policy.llm_usage_boundary: LLM may rewrite wording and parse user answers. LLM must not freely decide which unrelated fields to group together. Group selection is rule/planner controlled.

planner_scoring_policy.description: ActiveQuestionPlanner should select exactly one next group. The score chooses the next business decision, not the largest group of missing fields.
must_follow_order_rules: Ask channel before channel-specific fields such as sender, app_name, router, cost_center_id, or bounce periods.
Ask app_name before PUSH router/tag recommendation.
Ask delivery_mode and high_risk_flag before SMS router strategy when SMS is selected.
Ask Send_to_China_flag before CN SMS routing.
Ask bounce_back_next_channel before unknown_bounce_back_status and channel-specific bounce periods.
Ask project ownership fields near final review unless source_system is required for an earlier dependency.

answer_preview_policy.user_facing_preview: Show only direct business updates that the user explicitly answered or confirmed.
answer_preview_policy.internal_impact_plan: Store dependent changes such as fields becoming not applicable, required_now changes, option filtering, generated defaults, and cleanup operations.
answer_preview_policy.preview_wording: Use display_name and business meaning, not raw internal field names, unless the field name is the only clear label.

question_groups.title: Review differences from reference
question_groups.user_goal: Help the user understand and confirm important differences between the selected historical use case and the new requirement.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask about one high-impact difference at a time. If the conflict is about channel, route to qg_channel_priority after explaining the reference difference.
ask_when: there is at least one UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT field
skip_when: no reference snapshot exists
all reference conflicts are resolved
conflict is only low-priority final ownership info
question_groups.question_template: I found an important difference from the reference use case. The reference has {reference_value} for {field_display_name}, while your new requirement suggests {requirement_value}. Should we update it for this new use case?
question_groups.assistant_context_template: This matters because {business_reason}. I will only apply the change after you confirm it.
answer_hints: You can answer with: keep the reference value, use the new requirement value, or specify another value.
If this difference is intentional, confirm the new value so I can update the draft.
accepted_answer_examples: Use both SMS and Email, Email mandatory and SMS priority 1.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show only the field that changes from reference to new confirmed value. Hide downstream dependent changes.

question_groups.title: Confirm business trigger
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm what business event triggers the message and what scenario this use case represents.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is only about business trigger/scenario. Do not ask project name, source system, owner, delivery mode, or 7*24 here.
ask_when: raw requirement is vague about trigger
skip_when: message_trigger_conditions is confirmed
mode = EDIT_REFERENCE and trigger inherited from reference with no conflict and no requirement override
question_groups.question_template: When should this message be sent? Please confirm the business event that triggers the message.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: I will use this to define the use case scenario and generate a clearer use case name if needed.
answer_hints: Examples: product maturity reminder, OTP request, card-not-present alert, transaction alert, eStatement/eAdvice ready notification, campaign launch.
A good answer mentions the event, customer/account situation, and why the message is sent.
accepted_answer_examples: Send when a customer's wealth product will mature in 30 days.
Send when an OTP is requested for login.
Send when an eStatement is ready on the website.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show trigger/scenario update and generated use case name only if the user explicitly accepts or clearly provides it.

question_groups.title: Confirm business identity
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm the high-level identity of the use case, especially business line and use case name.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is about how the use case is identified. Do not ask delivery setup or ownership details here.
ask_when: line_of_business is missing and cannot be derived from user department
use_case_name is missing or generated with low confidence
skip_when: line_of_business is prefilled with high confidence and use_case_name is confirmed
prerequisites: qg_business_scenario should be resolved or low-risk skipped
question_groups.question_template: How should this use case be identified? Please confirm the line of business and the use case name.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: The use case name is used for users to check related message delivery information in MDC MI reports.
answer_hints: Line of business options: WPB or CMB.
Use case name should describe the business scenario, not just the project name.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show line of business and use case name updates.

question_groups.title: Confirm delivery mode and risk
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm operational sending mode, risk level, and 7*24 requirement because these affect route strategy and delivery behavior.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is about operational behavior and risk only. Do not ask sender, router, project name, or owner here.
ask_when: SMS router recommendation needs delivery_mode or high_risk_flag
skip_when: delivery_mode, high_risk_flag, and delivery_schedule are confirmed
none of the missing fields are required_now and no selected channel depends on them
question_groups.question_template: How should this message be delivered from an operation and risk perspective? Please confirm the delivery mode, whether it is high-risk, and whether 7*24 delivery is required.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: This affects router recommendation and traffic split. For example, Time Critical usually maps to TC routes, Realtime maps to RT routes, and high-risk SMS may require dual-vendor routing.
answer_hints: Delivery mode options: Time Critical, Realtime, Batch.
High-risk examples: regulatory message, time-sensitive message, Card-Not-Present message, high-risk transaction message.
7*24 means the delivery schedule supports around-the-clock sending.
accepted_answer_examples: Realtime, high-risk because it is a card-not-present transaction, 7*24 required.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show these three business decisions if changed. Router and traffic changes remain hidden recommendations or internal impact until confirmed separately.

question_groups.title: Confirm delivery channel
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm which channels the message should use and how priority should be represented in useCaseChannelRuleList.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group selects channels and priority only. Do not ask router, sender, app name, cost center, traffic percentage, or bounce-back here.
skip_when: channel is confirmed and every selected channel has priority or default priority is accepted
question_groups.question_template: Which delivery channel should this use case use? If multiple channels are needed, please also provide the sending priority.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: I will create one channel rule item for each selected channel. Priority 0 means mandatory send, 1-8 means sending priority, and 99 means optional / may not send.
answer_hints: Available channels: SMS, Email, Push, Letter, WhatsApp, WeChat, Two-way SMS, MMS.
Example: Email mandatory and Push priority 1.
Do not provide router or sender yet unless you already know them; I will ask channel-specific setup next.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show channel rule list changes. Hide downstream applicability changes for channel-specific fields.

question_groups.title: Confirm Email setup
question_groups.user_goal: Collect or confirm Email route and sender setup using business-friendly hints.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask Email-specific route and sender information. Do not ask SMS cost center, Push app, or generic ownership fields.
ask_when: EMAIL router is missing or recommendation needs confirmation
sender_name is required because EMAIL router contains PFP
skip_when: channel does not contain EMAIL
EMAIL router, sender, and required sender_name are confirmed
question_groups.question_template: For Email delivery, is this message for external customers or internal users? Please also provide the sender domain or sender address if known.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: This helps recommend the Email router. New external email use cases normally use PFP_EU_EMAIL, while new internal email use cases normally use INT_EMAIL.
answer_hints: For sender, this is mainly the sender domain or sender address.
Only the self-identifiable part can usually be customized, for example **hangseng**@mail.test.hangseng.com.
If the Email router contains PFP, Sender Name is required. Most HASE cases use Hang Seng Bank / 恒生银行.
accepted_answer_examples: External customers, use PFP_EU_EMAIL, sender is noreply.wealth@hase.com.hk, sender name Hang Seng Bank.
Use PFP_EU_EMAIL and sender domain wealth@hase.com.hk.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show confirmed Email route/sender changes. Hide option filtering and PFP sender-name requiredness as internal impacts.

question_groups.title: Confirm SMS routing strategy
question_groups.user_goal: Determine SMS routing strategy based on China delivery, risk, and delivery mode.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is about SMS routing strategy only. Do not ask SMS sender ID or cost center here unless the user provides them voluntarily.
skip_when: channel does not contain SMS
SMS routing strategy is confirmed
prerequisites: delivery_mode and high_risk_flag known or intentionally skipped
question_groups.question_template: For SMS delivery, should this message be sent to China mobile numbers? This affects the SMS route and traffic split.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: If Send_to_China is Yes, the usual route is CM + LX with CM 100% and LX 0%. If high-risk, the usual route is HTCL + CSL dual vendor with HTCL 100% and CSL 0%. Otherwise, HTCL is usually selected based on delivery mode, such as TC or RT.
answer_hints: For Time Critical messages, TC router is usually relevant.
For Realtime messages, RT router is usually relevant.
For high-risk SMS, dual-vendor routing may be recommended.
accepted_answer_examples: No China mobile numbers; non-high-risk; use HTCL realtime route.
Yes, send to China. Use CM 100% and LX 0%.
High-risk realtime SMS; use HTCL and CSL, HTCL 100%, CSL 0%.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show confirmed SMS routing strategy. Hide suppressed legacy routers and option filtering.

question_groups.title: Confirm SMS sender and billing
question_groups.user_goal: Collect SMS sender ID and cost center ID after SMS is selected.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask SMS sender and billing information. Do not ask routing strategy here unless missing and no routing group will be asked.
skip_when: channel does not contain SMS
SMS sender and cost_center_id are confirmed
question_groups.question_template: For SMS, please provide the SMS sender ID and cost center ID.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: The cost center ID is required when SMS is selected.
answer_hints: Example cost center ID: 25267613.
For SMS, sender means SMS sender ID number.
accepted_answer_examples: Cost center 25267613; sender ID not confirmed yet.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show SMS sender and cost center updates.

question_groups.title: Confirm Push app and route
question_groups.user_goal: Identify Push app and generate the correct Push route recommendations.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask Push app, router, and tag. Do not ask Push callback/bounce-back period here.
ask_when: Push tag is required or missing
skip_when: channel does not contain PUSH
app_name and required Push route/tag setup are confirmed
question_groups.question_template: Which app should receive the Push Notification?
question_groups.assistant_context_template: This determines the Push route. DaaSC usually creates 3 routes: IOS with AWS-SG, AOS with AWS-SG, and AOS with AURORA. InvestEx or DBB usually creates 1 AWS-SG route.
answer_hints: Available app names: DaaSC, InvestEx, DBB.
For DaaSC, tag options include IOS and AOS.
For new Push use cases, ICCM-related Push routers are normally not selected.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show Push app and selected/recommended route changes. Hide suppressed router options.

question_groups.title: Confirm Push callback
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm whether MDC should wait for mobile callback for Push Notification.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask Push callback and Push callback waiting period. Do not ask next-channel fallback policy here unless user mentions it.
ask_when: bounce_back = Y and push_bounce_back_period is missing
skip_when: channel does not contain PUSH
bounce_back is confirmed and push_bounce_back_period is not required or confirmed
question_groups.question_template: Should MDC wait for a mobile callback for this Push Notification?
question_groups.assistant_context_template: If enabled, mobile will call MDC API after receiving the push notification. If MDC does not receive the callback within a preset period, it can trigger bounce-back behavior.
answer_hints: A typical waiting period example is 15 minutes, but use the value confirmed by the business.
This is mainly relevant when Push is selected.
accepted_answer_examples: Yes, wait for callback for 15 minutes.
Enable callback, period not confirmed yet.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show Push callback decision. Hide dependent next-channel fields unless user explicitly answered them.

question_groups.title: Confirm Letter route
question_groups.user_goal: Select Letter router based on auto-trigger or manual-trigger behavior.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only ask Letter route decision. Do not ask non-Letter bounce periods here.
skip_when: channel does not contain LETTER
LETTER router is confirmed
question_groups.question_template: For Letter delivery, is it auto-triggered or manually triggered?
question_groups.assistant_context_template: This affects the Letter router. Auto trigger normally uses OTX_BAT_LETTER, while manual trigger normally uses OTX_BAT_HTML_LETTER.
answer_hints: If the metadata uses OTX_BATCH_LETTER and OTX_BAT_LETTER differently, confirm the final router value with MDC IT.
Letter bounce-back success flag is mainly for INHK eStatement/eAdvice cases.

question_groups.title: Confirm other channel route
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm routes for channels whose routing is mostly deterministic or has very few options.
question_groups.decision_boundary: Only applies to MMS, WhatsApp, WeChat, and Two-way SMS. Do not use this group for SMS, EMAIL, PUSH, or LETTER.
ask_when: channel contains MMS or WHATSAPP or WECHAT or TWOWAYSMS
router missing for those selected channels
skip_when: no simple-route channel is selected
all simple-route channels have confirmed router
question_groups.question_template: Please confirm the route for the selected non-SMS/Email/Push channel.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: Some channels have standard route recommendations, such as HTCL_MKT_MMS for MMS, HARO_RT_WHATSAPP for WhatsApp, TENCENT_WECHAT for WeChat, and HTCL_2WAY_SMS for Two-way SMS.
answer_hints: If the standard recommendation is acceptable, you can simply say 'use the recommended route'.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show route selection for the selected channel.

question_groups.title: Confirm bounce-back to next channel
question_groups.user_goal: Confirm whether MDC should trigger the next channel when the current channel does not return the expected delivery status.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is about next-channel fallback and unknown status handling. Do not ask Push callback here unless it is directly mentioned.
ask_when: unknown_bounce_back_status is missing after bounce_back_next_channel = Y
user mentions fallback, retry, next channel, or unknown status
skip_when: only one channel selected and user did not mention fallback
bounce_back_next_channel = N and confirmed
question_groups.question_template: If the first channel does not return the expected delivery status, should MDC trigger the next channel?
question_groups.assistant_context_template: If yes, please confirm whether unknown status should be treated as failed or successful. You may also provide waiting periods in minutes for SMS, Email, Letter, or WeChat if known.
answer_hints: Unknown status as failed means MDC may trigger next channel when success is not confirmed.
Unknown status as successful means MDC treats the missing status as acceptable and may not trigger fallback.
Channel-specific waiting periods can be provided later if not known now.
accepted_answer_examples: Yes, trigger next channel. Treat unknown as failed. SMS wait 15 minutes and Email wait 30 minutes.
Yes, but period not confirmed yet.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show fallback decision and unknown-status handling. Period fields are shown only if the user explicitly provides them.

question_groups.title: Confirm project and ownership
question_groups.user_goal: Collect required project and owner fields near final review without interrupting core delivery setup.
question_groups.decision_boundary: This group is for final completion. Do not mix it with trigger, channel, routing, sender, or bounce-back questions.
ask_when: core delivery setup is mostly complete
skip_when: project_name, source_system, and message_owner are confirmed and department fields are prefilled or not required_now
prerequisites: core delivery groups completed or intentionally skipped
question_groups.question_template: Before finalizing, please provide the project name, source system, and message owner if known.
question_groups.assistant_context_template: Department head, team head, and business line fields can usually be filled from department or access information during final review.
answer_hints: Source system means the system that will pass the notification request to MDC.
Message owner should be GCB 5 or above according to the metadata.
Project name is different from use case name; project name identifies the delivery project, while use case name describes the message scenario.
accepted_answer_examples: Project ABC, source system PEGA, owner Chan Tai Man.
Source system is CMS; project name and owner to be confirmed.
Message owner is Jane Wong, GCB5.
question_groups.action_preview_policy: Show project and ownership updates. Do not trigger unrelated channel changes.
